channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Government Reverts England Bus Fare Cap to £2 from January
item.description: The UK government has announced that bus fares in England will revert to a £2 cap from January 2025, following a policy reversal by new Prime Minister Andy Burnham. The measure, expected to cost over ...

item.title: High School Journalism Project Sparks Legal Dispute Over Epstein Files
item.description: Ben Mueller, a high school senior, created an Instagram slideshow for his journalism class analyzing the Epstein files, linking Marin County towns to Jeffrey Epstein's connections. His post, which hig...

item.title: US naval vessels retreat from Red Sea amid heightened tensions with Iran
item.description: The article details the recent developments in the Red Sea where US naval ships have turned back due to heightened tensions with Iran. President Donald Trump has vowed to retaliate against Iran by tar...

item.title: Nairobi Taxi Driver Records Men Burning Flowers at Langata Cemetery
item.description: A Nairobi taxi driver captured footage of a strange ritual at Langata Cemetery where men in religious attire removed and burned flowers placed on a grave. The video, shared online, has sparked debate....

item.title: Pakistan and Iran Strengthen Security and Border Cooperation to Enhance Regional Stability
item.description: Federal Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi and Iranian counterpart Eskandar Momeni held high-level talks in Islamabad, reaffirming their nations' commitment to bolster bilateral security collaboration. Th...

item.title: Canada-U.S. Agreement on Gordie Howe Bridge Deal Sparks Political Debate
item.description: Ottawa has released the text of a controversial agreement with the U.S. to open the Gordie Howe International Bridge near Windsor, Ont., which has become a focal point of political tensions. The deal,...

item.title: South Africa's Home Affairs Department Launches Digital Transformation to Streamline Immigration and Identity Services
item.description: Home Affairs Minister Leon Schreiber announced accelerated digital reforms to modernize South Africa's immigration and identity systems. The initiative focuses on reducing bureaucratic hurdles through...

item.title: Indonesia Awards 12 Tanzanians with Master's Degrees in Global Scholarship Program
item.description: Indonesia has recognized 12 Tanzanian scholars with Master's degrees through a global scholarship initiative, highlighting collaborative efforts to enhance educational opportunities. The program, whic...

item.title: Philippine Authorities Seize Smuggled Cigarettes in Zamboanga and Lanao
item.description: Philippine authorities have successfully intercepted large quantities of smuggled cigarettes in two separate operations across Zamboanga Sibugay and Lanao del Norte. In Zamboanga Sibugay, the Naval Co...

item.title: White House Considers Military Action in Mali Amid Russian Ties and Internal Debate
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration is facing internal turmoil as senior officials debate whether to authorize military strikes against Jama'at Nusrat al-Islam wal-Muslimin (JNIM), an al-Qaeda-lin...

item.title: Tanzanian man sentenced to life imprisonment for sexually abusing four children
item.description: Abdul Suleiman, a Tanzanian man, has been sentenced to life imprisonment after being convicted of raping and sodomizing four female children aged between seven and nine. The High Court in Bukoba dismi...

item.title: Former Football Player Jay Feely Wins Arizona GOP Primary Amid Democratic Challenges
item.description: Jay Feely, a former Arizona Cardinals kicker and close ally of President Donald Trump, secured victory in Arizona’s 1st District GOP primary, a highly competitive battleground. The win comes as Democr...

item.title: Impeachment Trials and Financial Record Subpoenas: A Comparative Analysis
item.description: The article compares the impeachment trials of former Chief Justice Renato Corona (2012) and Vice President Sara Duterte (2026), focusing on the subpoena of their financial records. Both cases involve...

item.title: UK and Pakistan clash over deportation of convicted rapist Shabir Ahmed
item.description: The UK government's plan to deport Shabir Ahmed, a 73-year-old child rapist linked to the Rochdale grooming gang, has sparked intense political debate. Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood faces backlash fr...

item.title: Matt Damon discusses physical challenges of filming 'The Odyssey'
item.description: In a recent interview with Radio Andy host Andy Cohen, actor Matt Damon revealed the physical toll endured by the 'middle-aged' cast of Christopher Nolan's film 'The Odyssey'. The 55-year-old star adm...

item.title: Ethiopia Launches 10-Year Golden Visa for High-Net-Worth Investors
item.description: Ethiopia is set to launch a 10-year Golden Visa program aimed at attracting wealthy foreign investors. The initiative, announced by the Immigration and Citizenship Service (ICS), allows foreign nation...

item.title: Zimbabwean Suspect Ndodana Tshuma Appears in Court Amid Extradition and Firearm Charges
item.description: Ndodana Mkhanyisi Tshuma, a suspect in the triple murder of his wife and daughters in the UK, is set to appear in Johannesburg Magistrate's Court. The case involves extradition proceedings initiated b...

item.title: Shania Twain Reflects on Roots in New Album 'Little Miss Twain'
item.description: Shania Twain, the iconic Canadian country music artist, has shared how her new album 'Little Miss Twain' allowed her to reconnect with her roots and the beauty of northern Ontario, where she was raise...

item.title: Calls for heading restrictions after former Socceroo's CTE diagnosis
item.description: Former Socceroos captain Paul Wade has revealed a probable chronic traumatic encephalopathy (CTE) diagnosis, making him the first Australian footballer to disclose such a condition. Wade, who captaine...

item.title: UNN Announces Graduation of 12,895 Students and Infrastructure Upgrades
item.description: The University of Nigeria (UNN) is set to graduate 12,895 students in its 55th Convocation, with 168 receiving First Class honours. Vice-Chancellor Simon Ortuanya revealed this during a press briefing...
